// prisma/schema.prisma

generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client-js"
}

datasource db {
  provider = "postgresql"
  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// App models
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

model Profile {
  id                 String   @id @default(cuid())
  userId             String   @unique
  firstName          String?
  lastName           String?
  email              String?
  phone              String?
  location           String?
  professionalLinks  Json?
  headline           String?
  summary            String?
  targetRoles        String[]
  targetLocations    String[]
  workModePreference String?
  salaryPreference   Int?
  completionStatus   Int      @default(0)
  createdAt          DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt          DateTime @updatedAt

  experiences Experience[]
  educations  Education[]
  skills      Skill[]
}

model Experience {
  id           String         @id @default(cuid())
  profileId    String
  type         ExperienceType
  title        String
  organization String?
  startDate    DateTime?
  endDate      DateTime?
  isCurrent    Boolean        @default(false)
  description  String?
  createdAt    DateTime       @default(now())
  updatedAt    DateTime       @updatedAt

  profile Profile @relation(fields: [profileId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
}

model Education {
  id           String    @id @default(cuid())
  profileId    String
  institution  String
  degree       String?
  fieldOfStudy String?
  startDate    DateTime?
  endDate      DateTime?
  gpa          String?
  createdAt    DateTime  @default(now())
  updatedAt    DateTime  @updatedAt

  profile Profile @relation(fields: [profileId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
}

model Skill {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  profileId String
  name      String
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt

  profile Profile @relation(fields: [profileId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
}

model Job {
  id                String    @id @default(cuid())
  userId            String
  title             String
  company           String
  location          String?
  description       String?
  compensationNotes String?
  applicationDate   DateTime?
  stage             JobStage  @default(INTERESTED)
  priority          Boolean   @default(false)
  deadline          DateTime?
  recruiterNotes    String?
  customNotes       String?
  lastActivityAt    DateTime?
  outcomeAt         DateTime?
  createdAt         DateTime  @default(now())
  updatedAt         DateTime  @updatedAt

  stageHistory  JobStageHistory[]
  activities    JobActivity[]
  documentLinks JobDocumentLink[]
}

model JobStageHistory {
  id        String    @id @default(cuid())
  jobId     String
  fromStage JobStage?
  toStage   JobStage
  changedAt DateTime  @default(now())

  job Job @relation(fields: [jobId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
}

model JobActivity {
  id          String    @id @default(cuid())
  jobId       String
  type        String
  title       String
  description String?
  scheduledAt DateTime?
  roundType   String?
  completed   Boolean   @default(false)
  createdAt   DateTime  @default(now())
  updatedAt   DateTime  @updatedAt

  job Job @relation(fields: [jobId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
}

model Document {
  id        String         @id @default(cuid())
  userId    String
  type      DocumentType
  name      String
  category  String?
  status    DocumentStatus @default(DRAFT)
  isDeleted Boolean        @default(false)
  deletedAt DateTime?
  createdAt DateTime       @default(now())
  updatedAt DateTime       @updatedAt

  versions DocumentVersion[]
  jobLinks JobDocumentLink[]
}

model DocumentVersion {
  id            String   @id @default(cuid())
  documentId    String
  versionNumber Int
  content       String?
  fileUrl       String?
  createdAt     DateTime @default(now())

  document Document          @relation(fields: [documentId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  jobLinks JobDocumentLink[]
}

model JobDocumentLink {
  id                String   @id @default(cuid())
  jobId             String
  documentId        String
  documentVersionId String
  linkedAt          DateTime @default(now())

  job             Job             @relation(fields: [jobId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  document        Document        @relation(fields: [documentId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  documentVersion DocumentVersion @relation(fields: [documentVersionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@unique([jobId, documentVersionId])
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Enums
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

enum JobStage {
  INTERESTED
  APPLIED
  INTERVIEW
  OFFER
  REJECTED
  ARCHIVED
}

enum ExperienceType {
  EMPLOYMENT
  PROJECT
}

enum DocumentType {
  RESUME
  COVER_LETTER
  OTHER
}

enum DocumentStatus {
  DRAFT
  READY
  ARCHIVED
}

content.text:
Branch `chore/code-review` created. Below is the review. I scoped findings to what's actually shipped in Sprint 1 (app shell, dashboard, profile, jobs/profile/auth APIs) and skipped speculation about unwritten features.

---

# Dartly — Sprint 1 Code Review

## TL;DR

The codebase is in **good shape for Sprint 1**. Architecture is clean, the lib/ infra is genuinely thoughtful (logger redaction, ApiError pattern, rate limit abstraction), tests cover ownership/auth denial paths, and conventions in CLAUDE.md are mostly followed.

The most important issues to address before Sprint 2 are:
1. **Middleware uses `getSession()` instead of `getUser()`** — JWT not validated server-side.
2. **No input validation (zod) on any API route** — body shape is trusted blindly.
3. **CLAUDE.md claims RLS enforces isolation; in practice Prisma bypasses RLS** because it connects as the DB owner. The `rls-policies.sql` file exists but does nothing for app traffic.
4. **Double error logging + leaking 401s as `error` level** in every route.

Nothing here is a release blocker, but several are easy wins to land before more code piles on top.

---

## High Severity

### 1. `src/middleware.ts:38` — `getSession()` should be `getUser()`
Supabase docs explicitly warn that `getSession()` reads cookies without revalidating the JWT against the auth server. For server-side route protection it must be `supabase.auth.getUser()`. `requireAuth` already does this correctly — middleware should match.

### 2. RLS is theatre for Prisma traffic
`supabase/rls-policies.sql` is well-written, but Prisma connects via `DATABASE_URL` (typically the postgres role / pooler with BYPASSRLS), so `auth.uid()` is `NULL` and policies aren't enforced for any app query. CLAUDE.md says:

> All queries account-scoped via RLS (no manual user_id filtering in app code)

…but the actual code does the opposite — `services/jobs.ts:36`, `:61`, `:82` all manually filter by `userId`, which is the *correct* thing to do given Prisma's connection. **The doc is wrong, not the code.** Recommend either:
- Update CLAUDE.md to say "manual user_id scoping in services; RLS is defense-in-depth for direct supabase-js access only," or
- Switch to a request-scoped Postgres role + `SET LOCAL request.jwt.claims` to actually enforce RLS through Prisma.

Whichever you pick, the current "we have RLS" claim is misleading and a future contributor will trust it and skip the manual scoping.

### 3. No request body validation on any API route
- `api/jobs/route.ts:26` destructures `title, company, location, stage, priority` from raw JSON. `stage` is then `as JobStage` cast in `services/jobs.ts:44` — an attacker passing `stage: "WHATEVER"` resolves to `undefined` from `STAGE_UI_TO_PRISMA`, gets cast to `PrismaJobStage`, and Prisma errors at the DB layer (logged as 500). Same for `priority` (no boolean check), and `title`/`company` are unbounded strings.
- `api/profile/route.ts:25` passes the entire body straight into `upsertProfile` with no schema check. `professionalLinks` is `unknown` → cast to `Record<string, string>` in `services/profile.ts:94`. A client can pass nested objects, arbitrary keys, anything.

You already have zod installed. Add `z.object(...).safeParse(body)` at the route boundary and return 400 on failure. This is a one-afternoon fix and will eliminate a whole class of bugs/exploits.

### 4. `services/profile.ts:161-206` — destructive replace on every PUT
On every profile save, *all* `experiences` / `educations` / `skills` rows for the user are deleted and recreated. Consequences:
- IDs are not stable (the Prisma IDs get regenerated, but the front-end keeps the old IDs in state until refresh).
- Any future foreign keys pointing at `Experience.id` / `Education.id` / `Skill.id` will break.
- Concurrent edits to different sections of the profile race each other (PATCH skills while PATCH experience runs → data loss).
- Profile completion / activity history / anything that wants stable IDs is impossible.

For Sprint 1 it works, but plan to refactor to upsert-by-id with a "delete missing IDs" diff before adding any feature that depends on stable child rows.

---

## Medium Severity

### 5. Double error logging and noisy 401s
Every API route does:
```ts
} catch (err) {
  logger.error("Failed to fetch jobs", { err });
  return handleApiError(err);
}
```
Two issues:
- The logger file (`src/lib/logger.ts:159`) explicitly warns: *"JSON.stringify(Error) returns '{}' — never pass Error objects directly in meta."* These route-level logs are doing exactly that. Use `logError("Failed to fetch jobs", err)`.
- `handleApiError` already logs unknown errors. For `ApiError(401)` the route logs at `error` level even though it's a normal auth denial — your prod error stream will be flooded with 401s on every page load before login. Either drop the route-level catch entirely (let `withHttpLogging` handle it) or only log when `!(err instanceof ApiError)`.

### 6. `src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts:28` — emails logged in plaintext
`logger.info("User logged in", { email })`. The redactor in `logger.ts:33` doesn't include `email`/`phone`. Emails are PII; under FERPA/GDPR they shouldn't sit in `combined.log`. Either redact or log a hashed user identifier.

### 7. `services/auth.ts:8` — fragile error string matching
```ts
if (error.message.includes("already registered")) { ... }
```
Supabase rotates these strings. Check `error.code` / `error.status` instead, or fall through to a generic 400. Otherwise a Supabase update silently turns conflict into "Bad Request."

### 8. `services/auth.ts:4` — register flow doesn't surface email confirmation state
`signUp()` returns `data.session === null` when email confirmation is required. The route returns `{ success: true, status: 201 }` regardless and the form does `router.push("/profile")` — which then gets bounced back to `/login` by middleware. Either disable email confirmation in Supabase, or branch on `data.session` and render a "check your email" screen.

### 9. Middleware matcher excludes `/api`
`config.matcher = "/((?!api|_next/...).*)"` means API routes have zero middleware coverage. You correctly rely on `requireAuth()` in every route, but it's worth being explicit in CLAUDE.md so that nobody adds an unprotected `/api/whatever/route.ts` thinking middleware has their back.

### 10. `src/lib/env.ts` doesn't include Supabase vars
The whole point of `env.ts` is centralized validation, but `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` and `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_DEFAULT_KEY` are checked at runtime in three different places (`middleware.ts:13`, `supabase-server.ts:5`, `services/supabase.ts:3`). Move them into `envSchema` so a missing var fails at boot, not on first request.

### 11. `next.config.ts:10` — Supabase URL hardcoded in CSP
`connect-src 'self' https://jpcflgphtkkqtbqmksdc.supabase.co`. Hardcoding this means staging/PR previews on a different Supabase project will be blocked from talking to their backend. Read it from `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` and template it in.

---

## Low Severity / Polish

### 12. `src/app/(app)/layout.tsx:11` — `user!.id` non-null assertion
Middleware *should* guarantee a user, but the `!` is a smell. Either redirect inside the layout if `!user`, or use `requireAuth()` here too (it's already isomorphic for server components).

### 13. `src/app/(app)/dashboard/page.tsx:39` — fetch with no error branch on `.then()` failure
`.finally(() => setLoading(false))` is fine, but a network failure leaves `jobs` empty silently with no toast. Cheap to add.

### 14. `src/components/dashboard/job_form.tsx:39` — `crypto.randomUUID()` for new jobs is dead state
The API discards the client-provided ID and returns its own. The randomUUID is only used for the `jobs.some(j => j.id === job.id)` check in the parent, which is also wrong on edge cases (collision astronomically unlikely but the logic is conceptually muddled). Cleaner: track an `editingJob` boolean instead of inferring from id presence.

### 15. `src/app/(app)/settings/page.tsx` — entire page is unwired stubs
`email`/`notifications`/`appPreferences` are local state with no backend persistence; `onUpdatePassword={() => {}}`. That's fine for Sprint 1, but add a `// TODO(sprint-2): wire to /api/profile or /api/settings` so it's obvious to a reviewer that this is intentional.

### 16. `src/components/auth/login-form.tsx:24` — email validation regex is too loose
`email.includes("@") && email.includes(".")` accepts `"@."`. Use `type="email"` (the register form does) and let the browser validate.

### 17. `src/services/prisma.ts:5` — no graceful shutdown / no log config
Minor: consider `new PrismaClient({ log: ['error', 'warn'] })` so query errors land in your logger transports.

### 18. `src/lib/rate-limit.ts:30` — `getRateLimiter` mutates a module-level Map
Already documented as "in-memory only — won't survive multi-instance," but worth noting the cached limiter ignores subsequent option changes (line comment exists). Once you deploy to Vercel this becomes meaningfully wrong, not just suboptimal — Vercel functions are multi-instance even within a single deployment.

### 19. `src/components/dashboard/add_job_modal.tsx:24` — modal lacks Esc-to-close, focus trap, body scroll lock
Standard modal a11y. The `Modal` UI component (`src/components/ui/modal.tsx`) probably handles this — `add_job_modal.tsx` rolls its own instead of reusing it. Consolidate.

### 20. File naming inconsistency
`add_job_modal.tsx`, `job_card.tsx`, `job_card_list.tsx`, `job_form.tsx` use snake_case while every other component file (`identity-section.tsx`, `confirm-delete-modal.tsx`, etc.) uses kebab-case. Pick one — Biome won't catch it but a future grep for "job-card" will miss them.

### 21. `src/app/api/jobs/route.ts:46` — `lastActivityAt: new Date()` on every PUT
Even if nothing changed (e.g. user hits "Save" twice). Probably intentional, but worth confirming that "last activity" is meant to mean "last edit" rather than "last meaningful state change."

### 22. `src/services/profile.ts:94` — `professionalLinks as Record<string, string>` cast
Once #3 (zod validation) is in place this cast becomes safe. Until then it's a lie.

---

## What's Actually Good (worth keeping)

- **`src/lib/logger.ts`** — the redactor that mutates in-place to preserve Winston's internal Symbols is exactly the right call, and it's commented to explain why. Nice work.
- **`src/lib/api-error.ts`** + `requireAuth` — the `ApiError(statusCode, message)` pattern is clean and lets you throw from services instead of passing response objects around.
- **`src/tests/api/jobs-id.test.ts`** — explicit "ownership denial returns 404, not 403" test cases show you're thinking about IDOR. Good defensive instinct.
- **`src/services/profile.ts:149`** — wrapping the upsert in `prisma.$transaction` is the right call given the destructive child-replace pattern.
- **CSP headers in `next.config.ts`** — nontrivial CSP, `frame-ancestors 'none'`, sensible Permissions-Policy. More than most Sprint 1 projects ship.
- **`src/lib/rate-limit.ts`** — clean abstraction, documented Redis swap path, sensible defaults.

---

## Suggested Sprint 2 ordering

1. Fix middleware `getSession` → `getUser` (5 min, big correctness win).
2. Add zod schemas at every API route boundary (half day).
3. Reconcile CLAUDE.md vs reality on RLS — pick a story and document it (15 min).
4. Stop double-logging and don't log 401s as errors (15 min).
5. Move Supabase env vars into `lib/env.ts` schema (10 min).
6. Decide on the `email` confirmation flow before adding more auth surface area.
7. Refactor profile child-row replace to a stable-id diff before any feature depends on `Experience.id` / `Education.id` / `Skill.id`.

Everything else is polish you can pick off as you touch the surrounding files.

name: CI

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
  push:
    branches: [main]

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 10

    env:
      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://user:password@localhost:5432/test
      NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL: https://placeholder.supabase.co
      NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_DEFAULT_KEY: placeholder-key

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: latest

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Generate Prisma client
        run: bun prisma generate

      - name: Lint
        run: bun lint

      - name: Type check
        run: bun run type-check

      - name: Test
        run: bun run test

      - name: Build
        run: bun run build

content.text:
Did a second pass. Validated each original issue against the additional files (Modal, Toast, DatePicker, Select, all profile/section components, settings sub-components, auth layout, forgot/reset password forms, CI workflow). Findings:

# Second-Pass Review

## Validation of Original Issues

| # | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Confirmed** | `middleware.ts:38` `getSession()` — real issue. |
| 2 | **Confirmed** | RLS theatre stands. Prisma DATABASE_URL is the postgres role; `auth.uid()` is NULL through Prisma. |
| 3 | **Confirmed** | Zero zod validation on any route. |
| 4 | **Confirmed and worse than I said** | Same destructive-replace pattern is used by `experience-section.tsx:63`, `education-section.tsx:55`, `skills-section.tsx:49` — all generate `crypto.randomUUID()` IDs that the server immediately discards. The IDs are useless for the entire profile feature, not just jobs. |
| 5 | **Confirmed** | Double-logging stands. |
| 6 | **Confirmed** | Email PII logging stands. |
| 7 | **Confirmed** | Fragile string match stands. |
| 8 | **Confirmed with caveat** | Behavior depends on Supabase project's "Confirm email" setting. If it's OFF, the current flow works. If it's ON, registration is silently broken. Verify the project setting and document. |
| 9 | **Confirmed** | Middleware /api exclusion stands. |
| 10 | **Confirmed** | Supabase env vars missing from `env.ts` schema. |
| 11 | **Confirmed** | Supabase URL hardcoded in CSP. |
| 12 | **Confirmed** | `user!.id` in `(app)/layout.tsx`. |
| 13 | **Confirmed** | Dashboard fetch silent failure. Minor. |
| 14 | **Confirmed and broader** | The same dead-state randomUUID pattern is in 4 places, not just `job_form.tsx`. |
| 15 | **Wrong — much more serious than I said** | `account-section.tsx` has a *fully wired* password-change form (current/new/confirm fields, validation, error messages), but `(app)/settings/page.tsx:41` passes `onUpdatePassword={() => {}}`. **Users will type their old password, type a new one, click "Update Password", get no error, and still have the old password.** This is silent data-integrity theatre — should be hidden or disabled, not shipped as a no-op. Promote to High. |
| 16 | **Confirmed** | Login email regex stands. The `forgot-password-form.tsx:22` form repeats the same loose check (`includes("@") && includes(".")`). |
| 17 | **Confirmed** | Prisma log config polish, low. |
| 18 | **Confirmed** | Rate limiter on Vercel. |
| 19 | **Half right — needs correction** | `Modal` (`src/components/ui/modal.tsx`) **does** handle Escape, body scroll lock, focus trap, and click-outside. The bug is that `dashboard/add_job_modal.tsx` reimplements its own modal from scratch instead of reusing `Modal`, so the Add/Edit Job dialog has no a11y. The fix is "use `Modal`," not "add a11y to `Modal`." Correction below. |
| 20 | **Confirmed** | Snake/kebab inconsistency. |
| 21 | **Confirmed** | `lastActivityAt` always-bumped, minor. |
| 22 | **Confirmed** | `professionalLinks` cast, fixed when zod lands. |

**Bottom line: 21 of 22 valid; #15 was understated, #19 was half-right.** No fabrications.

---

## New High Severity (missed in pass 1)

### 23. Authenticated users can't use password reset links
`middleware.ts:45-47`:
```ts
if (session && isPublicRoute) {
  return NextResponse.redirect(new URL("/dashboard", request.url));
}
```
`/reset-password` is in `publicRoutes`. So if a user is logged in on Device A and clicks the password reset link from email on Device B (where they're also logged in, e.g. another browser tab), or even on the same device after the reset email arrives, the middleware bounces them straight to `/dashboard` before `ResetPasswordForm` ever runs. Password reset is then **impossible without first logging out**.

Fix: split the public list — `/reset-password` should bypass the "redirect-if-authed" branch (it can have a session because Supabase exchanges the recovery code for one). Or detect `?code=` in the URL and skip the redirect.

### 24. Settings password change is a silent no-op (promoted from #15)
See above. This is a credential-handling UI that pretends to work. Either gate it behind a feature flag, render it disabled with a "Coming soon" badge, or wire it. Shipping a non-functional password form is the kind of thing that gets reported as a security vulnerability by users who think their old password no longer works.

---

## New Medium Severity

### 25. No rate limiting on password reset email flow
`forgot-password-form.tsx:30` calls `supabase.auth.resetPasswordForEmail()` directly from the browser. There's no server route in front of it, so the existing `checkRateLimit` infrastructure isn't applied. An attacker can hit the page in a loop and spam reset emails to any address (email enumeration + mailbox harassment).

Supabase has project-wide email rate limits but they're coarse and shared. Best practice is to add a thin `/api/auth/forgot-password` route that calls the server-side Supabase client and uses your existing `checkRateLimit({ id: "api/auth/forgot-password", limit: 3, windowSecs: 600 })`.

Same issue for `reset-password-form.tsx:84` (`updateUser({ password })`) — direct browser call, no server-side rate limit.

### 26. `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL` has no fallback in reset redirect
`forgot-password-form.tsx:31`:
```ts
redirectTo: `${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL}/reset-password`
```
If the env var is unset (and it's marked optional in `env.ts`), this becomes the literal string `"undefined/reset-password"`, which Supabase will reject and the reset email never sends. Either make `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL` required or fall back to `window.location.origin`.

### 27. DatePicker shows month/year only — users can't see which day they picked
`date-picker.tsx:62` formats the trigger button as `format(selected, "MMM yyyy")`, but `DayPicker` is `mode="single"` (day-level precision). A user picks April 15, and the trigger displays "Apr 2026" — they can't tell whether they actually selected the right day, especially when reopening the picker later. Either change format to `"MMM d, yyyy"` or change the picker to month-only mode.

This affects every form that uses dates: job form last-activity, experience start/end, education start/end. It's the kind of bug that causes data-quality complaints later (everyone thinks they entered the right day; nobody can verify).

### 28. Profile services have zero direct test coverage
`src/tests/api/profile.test.ts` mocks `services/profile`, so the destructive `deleteMany` + `createMany` block in `services/profile.ts:161-206` has no test at all. Given it's the most dangerous code in the file (concurrent writes will lose data; the transaction protects atomicity but not lost-update semantics), it deserves an integration test against a real Postgres before any feature depends on stable child-row IDs.

---

## New Low Severity

### 29. `error.tsx:20` references `bg-background` which isn't defined
The codebase uses Tailwind v4 `@theme` and `bg-zinc-950`/`bg-zinc-900` everywhere else. `bg-background` is leftover from the shadcn convention and silently no-ops. Replace with `bg-zinc-950`.

### 30. `career-preferences-section.tsx:47` salary parse can produce NaN
```ts
salaryPreference: salary ? Number.parseInt(salary, 10) : undefined
```
`Number.parseInt("abc", 10)` → `NaN`, which `JSON.stringify` then turns into `null`. It happens to work, but it's accidental. Add an explicit `Number.isFinite` check or use `<input type="number">` (it does — but the value is still a string). Cheap fix.

### 31. `forgot-password-form.tsx:22` and `login-form.tsx:24` both use `email.includes("@") && includes(".")`
Same too-loose check, repeated. Either rely on `type="email"` (the register form does, the others don't), or extract a shared validator.

### 32. `reset-password-form.tsx` duplicates register's password rules verbatim
20 lines of password validation copy-pasted from `register-form.tsx:31-65`. Extract a `validatePassword(pw): string | null` helper used by both. Will become important once an "update password" flow is added to settings (issue #24).

### 33. `(auth)/layout.tsx` is a no-op fragment
```ts
export default function AuthLayout({ children }) { return <>{children}</>; }
```
Route groups don't require a layout. Delete the file.

### 34. `Profile.completionStatus` field is dead code
Schema defines `completionStatus: Int @default(0)` (`schema.prisma:31`), but `services/profile.ts` never reads or writes it, and the profile page computes completion client-side from `getCompletionFields()`. Either wire it up or remove from the schema in the next migration.

### 35. CI runs against placeholder Supabase env vars
`.github/workflows/ci.yml:20-21` sets `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=https://placeholder.supabase.co`. That's fine for build/lint/type-check (and currently, for tests, since they're all mocked). But once you add an integration test against real Supabase Auth, CI will fall over silently. Add a TODO comment so future-you doesn't lose an hour debugging.

### 36. `ExperienceForm` requires description even for projects
`experience-form.tsx:59` — `description` is mandatory. For an `EMPLOYMENT` row that's reasonable; for `PROJECT` it's debatable. UX/product call, not a bug, but flagging.

---

## Things I Re-checked and Cleared

- **`Modal` component** — already has Esc, body-scroll lock, focus management, click-outside, `aria-modal`. Good. Original issue #19 was misdirected; the real problem is `dashboard/add_job_modal.tsx` reinventing the wheel. Use `Modal` there.
- **`Toast` `aria-live="polite"`** — correct, won't interrupt screen readers. Good.
- **`Select` keyboard nav** — implements arrow keys, Enter, Escape, focus management. Solid.
- **`requireAuth` test coverage** — actually thorough, four meaningful cases. Good.
- **Job ownership tests** — explicit "ownership denial returns 404" cases. Good defensive instinct.
- **CSP `frame-ancestors 'none'`** — clickjacking covered. Good.
- **Logger `redact` mutation in place** — preserves Winston Symbols correctly, well-commented. Good.

---

## Updated Severity Roll-Up

**High (fix before Sprint 2):**
1. (#1) Middleware `getSession` → `getUser`
2. (#2) Reconcile RLS doc vs. reality
3. (#3) Add zod validation to all API routes
4. (#4) Profile destructive child replace
5. (#23) Reset password broken for authed users
6. (#24) Settings password change is a silent no-op

**Medium:**
- (#5) Stop double-logging / don't error-log 401s
- (#6) Stop logging emails plaintext
- (#7) Replace fragile error string match
- (#8) Verify email confirmation flow
- (#10) Move Supabase env into `env.ts` schema
- (#11) Read CSP Supabase URL from env
- (#25) Rate limit forgot/reset password
- (#26) `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL` fallback
- (#27) DatePicker day visibility
- (#28) Add integration test for profile upsert

**Low:** #9, #12, #13, #14, #16, #17, #18, #19 (corrected), #20, #21, #22, #29-#36.

Net change from pass 1: **+6 new issues, 0 false positives, 2 corrections (#15 understated, #19 misdirected).**

Plan written to `docs/plans/2026-04-06-sprint1-code-review-fixes.md`.

**Structure: 6 phases, one PR each, ordered for safe landing.**

| Phase | Scope | Issues addressed |
|---|---|---|
| 1. Auth & doc correctness | middleware getUser, env schema, CSP, CLAUDE.md RLS reconciliation | #1, #2, #10, #11 |
| 2. Input validation | `validateBody` helper + zod schemas for jobs/profile/auth + drop unsafe casts + tests | #3, #22 |
| 3. Logging hygiene | Stop double-logging, drop email from auth logs, fix Supabase error code check | #5, #7 (partial #6 — drop email, not redact) |

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Next.js 16 App Router (route groups, server components, client components, middleware)
   - Supabase Auth (SSR pattern, `getSession()` vs `getUser()` security distinction, RLS policies)
   - Prisma ORM (type-safe queries, transactions, deleteMany/createMany vs upsert diff)
   - Zod v4 (uses `zod/v4` import path, `z.prettifyError`, `safeParse`)
   - Winston logger (redaction, child loggers, `logError` helper pattern)
   - Rate limiting with `rate-limiter-flexible` (in-memory, IP-based, cached by id)
   - `ApiError` class + `handleApiError` pattern for consistent API error responses
   - `withHttpLogging` wrapper for all API routes
   - `requireAuth()` as the auth guard for all protected API routes
   - Row-Level Security (RLS) — exists in `supabase/rls-policies.sql` but bypassed by Prisma's postgres-role DATABASE_URL
   - React 19 + React Compiler (`reactCompiler: true` in next.config.ts)
   - Tailwind v4 `@theme` API
   - Biome (linter/formatter, replaces ESLint+Prettier)
   - Vitest + Testing Library for tests

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - **`src/middleware.ts`**: Uses `getSession()` instead of `getUser()` at line 38 — Supabase docs warn getSession doesn't revalidate JWT server-side. Also hardcodes Supabase env checks rather than using `lib/env.ts`. Matcher excludes `/api`.
   - **`src/lib/env.ts`**: Good fail-fast zod validation, but missing `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` and `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_DEFAULT_KEY`. These are checked ad-hoc in 3 different files.
   - **`src/lib/logger.ts`**: Well-implemented — redaction of sensitive keys mutates in-place (preserves Winston Symbols), silent in test env. `SENSITIVE_KEYS` doesn't include `email`. `logError` helper must be used instead of `logger.error(..., { err })` because `JSON.stringify(Error) = "{}"`.
   - **`src/lib/api-error.ts`**: Clean `ApiError(statusCode, message)` pattern. `handleApiError` logs unknown errors only (not ApiError). Good.
   - **`src/lib/requireAuth.ts`**: Correctly uses `supabase.auth.getUser()` (unlike middleware). Returns `User` or throws `ApiError(401)`.
   - **`src/app/api/jobs/route.ts`**: GET and POST. No zod validation — raw destructuring from `request.json()`. Double-logs errors (`logger.error` + `handleApiError`). Stage cast `as JobStage` unsafe.
   - **`src/app/api/jobs/[id]/route.ts`**: PUT and DELETE. Same double-logging. `updateJob` checks ownership (two-step find+update — safe). `deleteMany` with userId scope — safe.
   - **`src/app/api/profile/route.ts`**: GET and PUT. No zod validation on PUT. Passes raw body to `upsertProfile`. GET returns `{}` for missing profile (acceptable).
   - **`src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts`**: Rate limited (10/min). Logs `{ email }` — PII. Minimal validation (just empty check).
   - **`src/services/jobs.ts`**: `updateJob` does ownership check via `findFirst` then `update` — correct but two DB calls. `deleteJob` uses `deleteMany` with userId — single atomic op.
   - **`src/services/profile.ts`**: `upsertProfile` uses `$transaction` (correct). Destructive `deleteMany` + `createMany` for experiences/educations/skills on every PUT — loses ID stability. `professionalLinks as Record<string,string>` unsafe cast.
   - **`src/services/auth.ts`**: `registerUser` checks `error.message.includes("already registered")` — fragile string match. Should use `error.code`/`error.status`.
   - **`src/services/prisma.ts`**: Standard singleton pattern. Missing log config.
   - **`prisma/schema.prisma`**: `Profile.completionStatus Int @default(0)` is never read/written in app code. Dead field.
   - **`next.config.ts`**: Supabase URL hardcoded in CSP: `https://jpcflgphtkkqtbqmksdc.supabase.co`. Should be read from env.
   - **`src/app/(app)/layout.tsx`**: `user!.id` non-null assertion at line 11. Should check `if (!user) redirect("/login")`.
   - **`src/app/(app)/dashboard/page.tsx`**: Full client component (uses `"use client"`). Fetch in `useEffect` with no error toast on network failure.
   - **`src/app/(app)/profile/page.tsx`**: Same pattern. `setProfile(profile)` rollback on save failure works because `profile` is closure-captured prev state.
   - **`src/app/(app)/settings/page.tsx`**: Passes `onUpdatePassword={() => {}}` — AccountSection shows full password change form but it silently does nothing. Critical UX bug.
   - **`src/components/ui/modal.tsx`**: Good — handles Esc, body scroll lock (`document.body.style.overflow = "hidden"`), focus on open via `dialogRef.current?.focus()`, `aria-modal="true"`. This was what #19 was wrong about.
   - **`src/components/ui/date-picker.tsx`**: `format(selected, "MMM yyyy")` on line 62 only shows month/year. Picker is day-mode. Users can't see which day they picked.
   - **`src/components/dashboard/add_job_modal.tsx`**: Rolls its own modal — no Esc, no scroll lock, no focus trap. Should use the `Modal` component.
   - **`src/components/auth/forgot-password-form.tsx`**: Calls `supabase.auth.resetPasswordForEmail()` directly from browser — no rate limiting. `${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL}/reset-password` has no fallback if env missing.
   - **`src/app/(auth)/layout.tsx`**: Empty pass-through fragment, can be deleted.
   - **`src/app/error.tsx`**: `bg-background` class doesn't exist in project's Tailwind theme. Should be `bg-zinc-950`.
   - **`supabase/rls-policies.sql`**: RLS policies DO exist and are well-written. But Prisma's postgres-role connection means `auth.uid()` is NULL for all Prisma queries — policies never fire for app traffic.
   - **`.github/workflows/ci.yml`**: Placeholder env vars for Supabase (fine for current mocked tests). No DB service container. Should have a comment.
   - **`docs/plans/2026-04-06-sprint1-code-review-fixes.md`**: Created — the full remediation plan.

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **Tool call cancellation**: When running parallel tool calls in one batch, if one Bash call fails (e.g., `ls /supabase/migrations` not found), subsequent parallel reads were cancelled. Resolved by re-running reads in a separate message.
   - **Issue #19 (original review)**: Originally said "Modal lacks Esc/scroll lock/focus trap." Second pass revealed this was WRONG — `modal.tsx` has all of these. The real bug is `add_job_modal.tsx` not using `Modal`. Corrected in second pass.
   - **Issue #15 (original review)**: Said "settings page is unwired stubs — add TODO comments." Second pass revealed this is far worse — `AccountSection` has a FULLY WIRED password change form (complete with validation, error messages, state) that silently does nothing because `onUpdatePassword={() => {}}`. Promoted to High severity (#24).

   - Net result: 0 false positives.
   - RLS vs Prisma analysis: RLS policies exist but are bypassed. CLAUDE.md claims RLS handles isolation but code manually filters by userId — the code is right, the doc is wrong.

1	# Sprint 1 Code Review — Remediation Plan
2	
3	> **IMPORTANT:** This plan document is for reference only. Do NOT commit this file. `docs/plans/` is in `.gitignore`.
4	
5	**Goal:** Resolve the issues identified in the Sprint 1 code review on branch `chore/code-review`. Out of scope: anything in the password-reset flow (forgot/reset password) and anything email-delivery-related — those will be addressed in a separate Sprint 2 plan.
6	
7	**Scope note — what is excluded:**
8	- Reset password middleware bug (#23)
9	- Rate limiting forgot/reset password (#25)
10	- `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL` fallback in reset email (#26)
11	- Email PII redaction in logger (#6)
12	- Supabase email confirmation flow verification (#8)
13	- Forgot/reset password form duplication (#31, #32)
14	
15	Everything else from the review is in scope here.
16	
17	**Working branch:** `chore/code-review` (already created). Land this work as 5–7 small PRs, one per phase, so each is reviewable in isolation.
18	
19	---
20	
21	## Phase ordering rationale
22	
23	Phases are ordered so each phase compiles and ships independently, and so the most important fixes (auth correctness, input validation, doc/reality reconciliation) land first while you still have the review fresh in your head.
24	
25	1. **Phase 1 — Auth & doc correctness** (small, high signal)
26	2. **Phase 2 — Input validation everywhere** (largest phase, blocks Phase 4)
27	3. **Phase 3 — Logging hygiene** (small, mechanical)
28	4. **Phase 4 — Profile child-row stability** (the hardest, depends on Phase 2)
29	5. **Phase 5 — UX bugs and dead UI** (settings password, DatePicker, dashboard)
30	6. **Phase 6 — Cleanup, naming, dead code** (mop-up, do last)
31	
32	---
33	
34	# Phase 1 — Auth & Doc Correctness
35	
36	Small, high-leverage fixes. Aim: one PR.
37	
38	## Task 1.1 — Middleware: `getSession()` → `getUser()`
39	
40	**File:** `src/middleware.ts:38-39`
41	
42	**Why:** `getSession()` reads cookies and trusts the local JWT signature without revalidating against the auth server. Supabase docs explicitly warn against using it for server-side route protection. `getUser()` round-trips to the auth server and catches revoked sessions, password changes, etc.
43	
44	**Change:**
45	```ts
46	const {
47	  data: { user },
48	} = await supabase.auth.getUser();
49	
50	if (!user && !isPublicRoute) {
51	  return NextResponse.redirect(new URL("/login", request.url));
52	}
53	
54	if (user && isPublicRoute) {
55	  return NextResponse.redirect(new URL("/dashboard", request.url));
56	}
57	```
58	
59	**Validate:**
60	- `bun run build` passes
61	- Manual: log in, navigate to /dashboard → no redirect. Log out, navigate to /dashboard → redirected to /login. Navigate to /login while authed → redirected to /dashboard.
62	
63	## Task 1.2 — Move Supabase env vars into `lib/env.ts` schema
64	
65	**Files:**
66	- `src/lib/env.ts` — add fields
67	- `src/middleware.ts:13-18` — use `env`
68	- `src/lib/supabase-server.ts:5-11` — use `env`
69	- `src/services/supabase.ts:3-9` — use `env`
70	
71	**Why:** The whole point of `lib/env.ts` is fail-fast validation at boot. Right now Supabase URL/key are checked at runtime in three places, each with its own error message.
72	
73	**Caveat:** `services/supabase.ts` runs in the browser (it's the browser client), so it can't import the server-only `lib/env.ts`. For browser code, keep direct `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_*` access. The validation in `env.ts` will still catch missing vars at server startup, which is enough — Next.js will inline the same vars into the client bundle at build time.
74	
75	**Change to `src/lib/env.ts`:**
76	```ts
77	const envSchema = z.object({
78	  // ...existing fields...
79	  NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL: z.url(),
80	  NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_DEFAULT_KEY: z.string().min(1),
81	});
82	```
83	
84	Then in `middleware.ts` and `supabase-server.ts`, replace the runtime check with `env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` / `env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_DEFAULT_KEY`.
85	
86	**Validate:**
87	- Build passes with vars set
88	- Build fails fast with a clear error if either var is missing (test by temporarily renaming in `.env.local`)
89	
90	## Task 1.3 — Read CSP Supabase URL from env
91	
92	**File:** `next.config.ts:10`
93	
94	**Why:** `connect-src 'self' https://jpcflgphtkkqtbqmksdc.supabase.co` hardcodes one Supabase project. Staging or PR previews on a different project will have all Supabase calls blocked by CSP.
95	
96	**Change:**
97	```ts
98	const supabaseHost = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL ?? "";
99	
100	// inside securityHeaders:
101	{
102	  key: "Content-Security-Policy",
103	  value: [
104	    "default-src 'self'",
105	    "script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'",
106	    "style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'",
107	    "img-src 'self' data: blob:",
108	    "font-src 'self'",
109	    `connect-src 'self' ${supabaseHost}`,
110	    "frame-ancestors 'none'",
111	  ].join("; "),
112	}
113	```
114	
115	**Validate:**
116	- Build passes
117	- Open DevTools → Network → confirm a request to Supabase succeeds without a CSP violation in the console
118	
119	## Task 1.4 — Reconcile RLS doc vs. reality in CLAUDE.md
120	
121	**File:** `CLAUDE.md` lines covering "Database conventions" and "Quality Checklist"
122	
123	**Why:** CLAUDE.md says *"All queries account-scoped via RLS (no manual user_id filtering in app code)"* — but the actual code in `services/jobs.ts` and `services/profile.ts` correctly does manual user_id scoping, because Prisma connects as the postgres role and bypasses RLS. The doc is wrong, not the code. A future contributor will trust the doc, skip the manual scoping, and ship an IDOR.
124	
125	**Decision required from Justin:** which story do you want to commit to?
126	- **(A)** Keep manual scoping in services. RLS is defense-in-depth only, applied to direct supabase-js queries. (Recommended — minimal change, matches current reality.)
127	- **(B)** Switch to a request-scoped Postgres role + `SET LOCAL request.jwt.claims` so Prisma actually enforces RLS. (Significant work, real per-row security.)
128	
129	**If (A), update CLAUDE.md:**
130	> Replace "All queries account-scoped via RLS (no manual user_id filtering in app code)" with:
131	> "All service-layer queries must explicitly filter by `userId` from `requireAuth()`. RLS policies in `supabase/rls-policies.sql` exist as defense-in-depth for any direct supabase-js calls (not used by Prisma)."
132	
133	Also remove the "RLS policies enforce account isolation in Supabase" line from the Quality Checklist or rephrase it as "manual user_id scoping in services + RLS defense-in-depth."
134	
135	**If (B):** out of scope for this plan; open a separate plan document.
136	
137	**Validate:** `grep -n "RLS" CLAUDE.md` shows updated language, no contradictions remain.
138	
139	---
140	
141	# Phase 2 — Input Validation Everywhere
142	
143	This is the largest phase. Goal: every API route validates its body with zod and returns 400 on failure. One PR.
144	
145	## Task 2.1 — Add a shared `validateBody` helper
146	
147	**New file:** `src/lib/validate-body.ts`
148	
149	```ts
150	import "server-only";
151	import type { NextRequest } from "next/server";
152	import type { z } from "zod/v4";
153	import { ApiError } from "@/lib/api-error";
154	
155	export async function validateBody<T extends z.ZodTypeAny>(
156	  request: NextRequest,
157	  schema: T
158	): Promise<z.infer<T>> {
159	  let raw: unknown;
160	  try {
161	    raw = await request.json();
162	  } catch {
163	    throw new ApiError(400, "Invalid JSON body");
164	  }
165	  const result = schema.safeParse(raw);
166	  if (!result.success) {
167	    throw new ApiError(400, z.prettifyError(result.error));
168	  }
169	  return result.data;
170	}
171	```
172	
173	**Why a helper:** keeps every route consistent (one error format), and lets `handleApiError` continue to be the single funnel.

399	# Phase 4 — Profile Child-Row Stability
400	
401	The hardest phase. Replaces the destructive `deleteMany` + `createMany` pattern with a stable upsert/diff. Depends on Phase 2 (zod-validated IDs). One PR.
402	
403	## Task 4.1 — Decide ID source
404	
405	The current client generates `crypto.randomUUID()` for new rows in `experience-section.tsx:63`, `education-section.tsx:55`, `skills-section.tsx:49`. The server then discards them and Prisma generates `cuid()`. Pick one:
406	
407	**Recommended:** server-generated. The client sends `id: undefined` (or `id: null`) for new rows; the server assigns `cuid()` and returns the full saved profile. Update the client to take the returned IDs and replace the temp UUIDs.
408	
409	**Schema change required:** update `ExperienceSchema`, `EducationSchema`, `SkillSchema` (Phase 2.2) so `id` is `z.string().optional()`.
410	
411	## Task 4.2 — Refactor `upsertProfile` to a stable diff
412	
413	**File:** `src/services/profile.ts:143-216`
414	
415	Replace the destructive block with a three-way diff per child collection:
416	
417	```ts
418	async function syncExperiences(
419	  tx: Prisma.TransactionClient,
420	  profileId: string,
421	  incoming: Experience[]
422	) {
423	  const existing = await tx.experience.findMany({
424	    where: { profileId },
425	    select: { id: true },
426	  });
427	  const existingIds = new Set(existing.map((e) => e.id));
428	  const incomingIds = new Set(incoming.filter((e) => e.id).map((e) => e.id!));
429	
430	  // Delete rows that are no longer present
431	  const toDelete = [...existingIds].filter((id) => !incomingIds.has(id));
432	  if (toDelete.length > 0) {
433	    await tx.experience.deleteMany({ where: { id: { in: toDelete } } });
434	  }
435	
436	  // Upsert each incoming row
437	  for (const e of incoming) {
438	    const data = {
439	      profileId,
440	      type: e.type,
441	      title: e.title,
442	      organization: e.organization || null,
443	      startDate: fromIsoDate(e.startDate),
444	      endDate: e.isCurrent ? null : fromIsoDate(e.endDate),
445	      isCurrent: e.isCurrent,
446	      description: e.description || null,
447	    };
448	    if (e.id && existingIds.has(e.id)) {
449	      await tx.experience.update({ where: { id: e.id }, data });
450	    } else {
451	      await tx.experience.create({ data });
452	    }
453	  }
454	}
455	```
456	
457	Repeat for educations and skills (skill is simpler — only `name` to compare).
458	
459	Then in `upsertProfile`:
460	```ts
461	return await prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => {
462	  const profile = await tx.profile.upsert({ ... });
463	
464	  if (data.experiences !== undefined) {
465	    await syncExperiences(tx, profile.id, data.experiences);
466	  }
467	  if (data.educations !== undefined) {
468	    await syncEducations(tx, profile.id, data.educations);
469	  }
470	  if (data.skills !== undefined) {
471	    await syncSkills(tx, profile.id, data.skills);
472	  }
473	
474	  const fresh = await tx.profile.findUnique({ ... });
475	  if (!fresh) throw new Error("Profile not found after upsert");
476	  return toProfileData(fresh);
477	});
478	```
479	
480	**Why a transaction is still needed:** the diff is read-then-write across multiple statements; without `$transaction`, two concurrent saves could each compute the same delete set and double-apply.

// prisma/schema.prisma

generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client-js"
}

datasource db {
  provider = "postgresql"
  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// App models
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

model Profile {
  id                 String   @id @default(cuid())
  userId             String   @unique
  firstName          String?
  lastName           String?
  email              String?
  phone              String?
  location           String?
  professionalLinks  Json?
  headline           String?
  summary            String?
  targetRoles        String[]
  targetLocations    String[]
  workModePreference String?
  salaryPreference   Int?
  completionStatus   Int      @default(0)
  createdAt          DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt          DateTime @updatedAt

  experiences Experience[]
  educations  Education[]
  skills      Skill[]
}

model Experience {
  id           String         @id @default(cuid())
  profileId    String
  type         ExperienceType
  title        String
  organization String?
  startDate    DateTime?
  endDate      DateTime?
  isCurrent    Boolean        @default(false)
  description  String?
  createdAt    DateTime       @default(now())
  updatedAt    DateTime       @updatedAt

  profile Profile @relation(fields: [profileId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
}

model Education {
  id           String    @id @default(cuid())
  profileId    String
  institution  String
  degree       String?
  fieldOfStudy String?
  startDate    DateTime?
  endDate      DateTime?
  gpa          String?
  createdAt    DateTime  @default(now())
  updatedAt    DateTime  @updatedAt

  profile Profile @relation(fields: [profileId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
}

model Skill {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  profileId String
  name      String
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt

  profile Profile @relation(fields: [profileId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
}

model Job {
  id                String    @id @default(cuid())
  userId            String
  title             String
  company           String
  location          String?
  description       String?
  compensationNotes String?
  applicationDate   DateTime?
  stage             JobStage  @default(INTERESTED)
  priority          Boolean   @default(false)
  deadline          DateTime?
  recruiterNotes    String?
  customNotes       String?
  lastActivityAt    DateTime?
  outcomeAt         DateTime?
  createdAt         DateTime  @default(now())
  updatedAt         DateTime  @updatedAt

  stageHistory  JobStageHistory[]
  activities    JobActivity[]
  documentLinks JobDocumentLink[]
}

model JobStageHistory {
  id        String    @id @default(cuid())
  jobId     String
  fromStage JobStage?
  toStage   JobStage
  changedAt DateTime  @default(now())

  job Job @relation(fields: [jobId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
}

model JobActivity {
  id          String    @id @default(cuid())
  jobId       String
  type        String
  title       String
  description String?
  scheduledAt DateTime?
  roundType   String?
  completed   Boolean   @default(false)
  createdAt   DateTime  @default(now())
  updatedAt   DateTime  @updatedAt

  job Job @relation(fields: [jobId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
}

model Document {
  id        String         @id @default(cuid())
  userId    String
  type      DocumentType
  name      String
  category  String?
  status    DocumentStatus @default(DRAFT)
  isDeleted Boolean        @default(false)
  deletedAt DateTime?
  createdAt DateTime       @default(now())
  updatedAt DateTime       @updatedAt

  versions DocumentVersion[]
  jobLinks JobDocumentLink[]
}

model DocumentVersion {
  id            String   @id @default(cuid())
  documentId    String
  versionNumber Int
  content       String?
  fileUrl       String?
  createdAt     DateTime @default(now())

  document Document          @relation(fields: [documentId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  jobLinks JobDocumentLink[]
}

model JobDocumentLink {
  id                String   @id @default(cuid())
  jobId             String
  documentId        String
  documentVersionId String
  linkedAt          DateTime @default(now())

  job             Job             @relation(fields: [jobId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  document        Document        @relation(fields: [documentId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  documentVersion DocumentVersion @relation(fields: [documentVersionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@unique([jobId, documentVersionId])
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Enums
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

enum JobStage {
  INTERESTED
  APPLIED
  INTERVIEW
  OFFER
  REJECTED
  ARCHIVED
}

enum ExperienceType {
  EMPLOYMENT
  PROJECT
}

enum DocumentType {
  RESUME
  COVER_LETTER
  OTHER
}

enum DocumentStatus {
  DRAFT
  READY
  ARCHIVED
}

name: CI

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
  push:
    branches: [main]

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 10

    env:
      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://user:password@localhost:5432/test
      NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL: https://placeholder.supabase.co
      NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_DEFAULT_KEY: placeholder-key

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: latest

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Generate Prisma client
        run: bun prisma generate

      - name: Lint
        run: bun lint

      - name: Type check
        run: bun run type-check

      - name: Test
        run: bun run test

      - name: Build
        run: bun run build

## Task 4.1 — Decide ID source

The current client generates `crypto.randomUUID()` for new rows in `experience-section.tsx:63`, `education-section.tsx:55`, `skills-section.tsx:49`. The server then discards them and Prisma generates `cuid()`. Pick one:

**Recommended:** server-generated. The client sends `id: undefined` (or omits `id`) for new rows; the server assigns `cuid()` and returns the full saved profile. The parent (`profile/page.tsx`) already overwrites local state with the server response after save, so the temp UUIDs naturally get replaced by canonical cuids on the next render.

**Schema change required:** in **this PR** (Phase 4), update `ExperienceSchema`, `EducationSchema`, `SkillSchema` from Phase 2.2 so `id` is `z.string().optional()`. This means Phase 2 lands with `id: z.string()` (required, matching the current client which still sends randomUUIDs), then Phase 4 loosens it to optional and removes the client-side UUIDs in the same PR. The two changes must ship together — do not loosen the schema in Phase 2 or you'll have a window where the server accepts ID-less rows but the client still sends them anyway, which is harmless but confusing for reviewers.
